converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Oscar Michel Tschirky papers
Tschirky, Oscar, 1866-1950.
3990
7 cubic feet. 7 cubic feet. 6 scrapbooks.,
ca.1890-1958. Collection material in English Includes six scrapbooks of clippings, telegrams, and correspondence on his career at the Waldorf-Astoria, ca. 1897-1950; one scrapbook of forms used at the hotel; two published booklets on staff functions; photographs of Oscar and his wife Sophie Bertisch Tschirky; and an etching and a sketch of the Waldorf-Astoria. Also scrapbooks, a guest book, a scrapbook of congratulatory letters from many famous personalities, and a tribute book given to the Tschirkys on their golden wedding anniversity, 1937. Scrapbooks of clippings, telegrams, correspondence, and forms; photographs, an etching, a sketch, a guest book, and a tribute book. 2070116
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Oscar Tschirky, Host of the Waldorf-Astoria, New York, was known as Oscar of the Waldorf.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes six scrapbooks of clippings, telegrams, and correspondence on his career at the Waldorf-Astoria, ca. 1897-1950; one scrapbook of forms used at the hotel; two published booklets on staff functions; photographs of Oscar and his wife Sophie Bertisch Tschirky; and an etching and a sketch of the Waldorf-Astoria. Also scrapbooks, a guest book, a scrapbook of congratulatory letters from many famous personalities, and a tribute book given to the Tschirkys on their golden wedding anniversity, 1937.
Also, photograph of the 1890 Supreme Court, signed by each Justice.
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
B. Huth
Date completed:
August 1982
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, April 2004
Date modified:
Jude Corina, July 2017
FAMOUS PERSONALITIES WHO WROTE CONGRATULATORY LETTERS TO OSCAR TSCHIRKY
Jules S. Bache
Bernard M. Baruch
Nicholas Murray Butler
Howard Chandler Christy
Walter P. Chrysler
Irvin S. Cobb
Grace Coolidge
Richard Crooks
Thomas E. Dewey
Charles Dana Gibson
J. Edgar Hoover
Fannie Hurst
Herbert H. Lehman
J. Harry Letsche
James H. McGraw
Henry Morgenthau
Conde Nast
Ethelbert Nevin
John J. Pershing
Ogden Reid
Edward V. Rickenbacker
Eleanor Roosevelt
Nellie Taylor Ross
Jacob Gould Schurman
Alfred P. Sloan
Edward R. Stettinius
Arthur Hays Sulzberger
William Sulzer
Myron C. Taylor
Walter C. Teagle
Thomas J. Watson
Felix M. Warburg
Owen D. Young
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: F-2361-2367, SR-34042-34046.
Cite As:
Oscar Michel Tschirky papers, #3990. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Scrapbooks. Guest books. Presidents. -- United States. Hotels, taverns, etc. -- New York (N.Y.) Tschirky, Sophie Bertisch. Oscar of the Waldorf. United States. Supreme Court (Date of work: 1890..) Waldorf-Astoria Hotel (New York, N.Y.)

No. 1 - Golden Wedding Anniversary Testimonial Dinner to Mr. & Mrs. Oscar Tschirky at Waldorf Astoria Hotel Side B [Staff access]15 photographs of Oscar and Sophie Tschirky and friends1Mounted tribute to the Tschirkys on their Golden Wedding Anniversary [see also leather bound tribute book]1Mounted sketch by "Ripley" [Believe It or Not] to Oscar of the Waldorf19331Matted etching of the Waldorf-Astoria, by [Andrea Laroy?]19291Booklet: "Behind the Scenes at the Waldorf-Astoria"19351Booklet: "The Waldorf-Astoria 50th Anniversary Staff Party" [pages stapled together out of order]19431Oscar Tschirky's scrapbook; clippings, NO COVER1897-19043Oscar Tschirky's scrapbook; clippings and correspondence1904-19291Oscar Tschirky's scrapbook; clippings1913-19292Oscar Tschirky's scrapbook; clippings, telegrams, articles on Tschirky, and correspondence19243Oscar Tschirky's scrapbook; clippings on Oscar, 50th anniversary of the Waldorf-Astoria, and opening of "Culinarians Home" in New Paltz, NY [later the Culinary Institute of America?]1924-19504Oscar Tschirky's scrapbook; clippings1937-19415Scrapbook of forms used in the Waldorf-Astoria, especially in the back of the house6Scrapbooks from the Tschirky's 50th Wedding Anniversary19377Leather-bound guest book "Golden Wedding Sophie and Oscar September 18, 1937" (includes photos)19378Leather bound tribute book, in mahogany case, with signatures of many famous personalities (F.D.R., John J. Pershing, Henry Luce, Fannie Hurst, Grace Moore, Alfred E. Smith, etc. etc.), includes seating plan for anniversary banquet2Leather bound scrapbook of letters of congratulations to the Tschirkys on their anniversary2Scrapbook of clippings and telegrams of congratulations of the Tschirkys' anniversary2Slides - 50th Anniversary, Waldorf Astoria, Mar. 15, 194319434Slides - Farm, Aug. party for Dad's 79th Birthday, Sept. 28, 194519454Slides - "Oscar of the Waldorf Suite" Raleigh Hotel, Washington, D.C., Apr. 7, 194319434Photo: Mrs. Antoinette Fassfind Tschirky, mother of Oscar Tschirky and grandmother of Leopold ('12)Correspondence, scrapbooks, photos, awards, clippings & menus5Correspondence, scrapbooks, photos, awards, clippings & menus6Correspondence, scrapbooks, photos, awards, clippings & menus7Photograph albums (8)8Recipes A-Z1930s91-4
Scope and Contents
Includes several famous dishes attributed to Oscar Tschirky, including typed versions of Waldorf Salad, Thousand Island dressing, and Eggs Benedict.Memos concerning the Waldorf-Astoria1936-193895-630th Anniversary of the Waldorf-Astoria1893-19239Day Book - events at the Waldorf-Astoria1893-18999Day book owned by Oscar1901-19029Book concerning the set up of the ballroom at the Waldorf9Waldorf-Astoria - book no. 2 - what was served9Herbert Hoover101Dwight D. Eisenhower102Menu - St. Andrew's Society Anniversary Dinner1854103William H. Taft1913104Franklin D. Roosevelt1933105Harry Truman106Calvin Coolidge107Signed poster for John J. Pershing honorary dinnerSeptember 10, 1919111Signed letter from Ministere des Affaires Etrangeres112Envelope with writing and profile of Tschirky1947112Photograph and articles on Oscar's Swiss chalet model113Newspaper articles1925-1958114Birthday celebration pamphlets1935-1937115Signed pamphlet and photograph by Eric Hauser1926116Menu notes and recipes, correct set up for table notes1915-1917117Pamphlets and advertisements1932-1942118Clippings from Redbook and Life1939119Photograph of Ye Pur Lee, Ching Dynasty ambassador18971110Personal letters1930-19501111Autographed photographs1112Award and medal from the French Minister of Agriculture1Photograph of the 1890 Supreme Court, signed by each Justice18901Portrait of Francis J. SpellmanUndated1Signed photo of a couple, signatures illegible except for "George"19411Photo of Waldorf-Astoria during paradeUndated1Photo - first annual "Oscar" dinner, Geneva Executive Club, Waldorf-Astoria1953-10-121Drawing of Madison Cottage printUndated1Photo - dinner offered by the Incorporated Association of Hotels and Restaurants to Mr. and Mrs. Oscar Tschirky, the Savoy Hotel, London1924-09-302New York Times article on Tschirky1937-10-032American National Red Cross certificate1916-07-012Societe Culinaire Philanthropique - certificate to Mr. and Mrs. Oscar Tschirky on their wedding anniversary - signed by president, vice president and secretaryUndated2Menu for "Narsai's" restaurantUndated2Honorary membership certificate - International Geneva Association1920-03-112 ======= Movie films7" reelsFarm, New Paltz, N.Y.F-23661938-19404
